title: Revise round-trip — feedback to live Foreman or re-claimed issue
description:
## What to build

Wire the human-relay revise action for branch-mode submissions so revision is a real round-trip rather than a glorified rejection. If the Foreman is still alive, revise sends feedback to it (over the existing supervisor-inbox `revision-requested` envelope) and the Foreman continues the Ralph Loop with the note. If the Foreman has already exited, human-relay leaves a note on the bd issue and re-claims (Kanban dispatches a fresh Foreman that picks up the note).

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Story #14); see also ADR-0005.

## HITL — what needs human review

- The "live vs exited" branching logic + which side owns the decision
- Note format on the bd issue when the Foreman has exited
- How the re-claimed Foreman discovers and reads the prior note

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] human-relay revise action on a branch submission requires a non-empty note
- [ ] If the Foreman process is alive, revise routes the note via `revision-requested` and the Foreman continues the loop
- [ ] If the Foreman has exited, revise appends the note to the bd issue and the issue returns to `bd ready`
- [ ] The next Foreman dispatched for that issue picks up and acts on the prior note
- [ ] Revision cap (existing 3-per-thread) preserved
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough for the live-Foreman path
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough for the exited-Foreman / re-claim path

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-l0u (approve / reject round-trip)

title: Foreman runs project tests; test status surfaces in submission
description:
## What to build

Foreman runs the Project's tests inside its worktree before submitting and populates the `testOutput` field on the branch-mode Submission payload. human-relay surfaces test status (pass / fail / not-run) in the approval UI so the human reviewer sees whether the Foreman self-verified before approving.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #9, #12); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Foreman recipe instructs the agent to run the Project's test command in its worktree
- [ ] Foreman captures test exit code + truncated stdout/stderr into `testOutput`
- [ ] Branch-mode submission payload includes `testOutput` with `{status: pass|fail|not-run, output: <truncated>}`
- [ ] human-relay renders test status visibly alongside approve / reject / revise
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough against a project with one trivial passing test: status shows `pass` in human-relay
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough against a project with a failing test: status shows `fail`

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-d0d (branch-mode submission payload)
- AgentFactory-p5o (Foreman recipe)

title: Foreman delegates to Workers via Atomic Delegate mid-loop
description:
## What to build

Verify the Foreman recipe can `delegate` to specialist Worker recipes (`code-reviewer`, `change-reviewer`, future `type-checker`, `doc-writer`) mid-Ralph-Loop using the existing Atomic Delegate primitive — unchanged from the deferred stack. Foreman recipe declares `agents: [code-reviewer, ...]` so the Habitat allowlist permits delegation. Workers do not interact with bd.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #20, #21); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Foreman recipe declares `agents:` listing at least one Worker recipe
- [ ] Worker recipes accessible from Ralph stack (in-place or copied/moved into `agents/ralph/` as appropriate)
- [ ] Atomic Delegate works unchanged — Foreman calls `delegate({recipe: "code-reviewer", ...})` mid-loop and the Worker submission queues on the Foreman's deferred-confirm rail
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough: Foreman runs Ralph Loop, calls `delegate` to a code-reviewer, receives review, continues the loop
- [ ] Workers do not call any bd commands

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-p5o (Foreman recipe)

title: Approval round-trip — approve closes bd, reject releases claim
description:
## What to build

Wire the human-relay approve / reject actions for branch-mode submissions to bd lifecycle and worktree cleanup. On approve: human-relay closes the bd issue (`bd close`) and disposes the worktree. On reject: human-relay releases the bd claim (so the issue becomes ready again) and disposes the worktree. Both paths exit the Foreman cleanly.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #11, #15, #27); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `human-relay` approve action on a branch submission closes the bd issue
- [ ] `human-relay` approve action disposes the worktree via `disposeWorktree`
- [ ] `human-relay` reject action releases the bd claim (issue returns to `bd ready`)
- [ ] `human-relay` reject action disposes the worktree
- [ ] Foreman exits cleanly after either action
- [ ] Aborted / timed-out Foreman also releases its claim and disposes its worktree
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough: create issue → Foreman submits → reject → issue is ready again, worktree gone
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough: create issue → Foreman submits → approve → issue closed, worktree gone

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-p5o (Foreman recipe)

title: Minimal Foreman recipe — Ralph Loop ships branch submission to human-relay
description:
## What to build

A new Foreman recipe under `pi-sandbox/agents/ralph/` that runs the Ralph Loop end-to-end against a bd issue: claim the issue, prepare a per-issue git worktree, make a minimal stub change, commit, and ship a branch-mode Submission to `human-relay`. Tier is `LEAD_HARE_MODEL`. Generous timeout (10–30 min). human-relay renders the branch payload with approve / reject / revise actions on the existing `respond_to_request` surface.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #7, #10, #11, #13, #16, #25, #31); see also ADR-0005.

## HITL — what needs human review

- Foreman prompt + tool palette (bash, fs tools, delegate, worktree-mgr)
- Loop shape: claim → worktree → change → commit → submit
- Timeout default within the 10–30 min target
- Tier choice (`LEAD_HARE_MODEL` per PRD)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pi-sandbox/agents/ralph/<foreman>.yaml` exists, model tier `LEAD_HARE_MODEL`
- [ ] Foreman tools palette: `bash`, `read`, `write`, `edit`, `grep`, `find`, `glob`, `delegate`
- [ ] Foreman calls `bd update <id> --claim` at session start
- [ ] Foreman calls `prepareWorktree` and runs inside the resulting worktree
- [ ] Foreman makes a stub change, commits to the feature branch with a coherent message
- [ ] Foreman ships a branch-mode `submission` envelope to `human-relay` with `branchRef`, `projectPath`, `issueId`
- [ ] human-relay renders the branch payload with approve / reject / revise actions visible
- [ ] Foreman session timeout configurable; default within 10–30 min
- [ ] End-to-end tmux walkthrough: create bd issue → mesh dispatches Foreman → branch submission appears in human-relay
- [ ] Header references PRD-0001 and ADR-0005

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-d0d (branch-mode submission payload)
- AgentFactory-nur (worktree manager)
- AgentFactory-1me (mesh launcher)

title: Mesh launcher — npm run mesh -- --project <path>
description:
## What to build

A new mesh-launch entry point `npm run mesh -- --project ~/Projects/myapp` that wires the Kanban + bd-watcher + human-relay against a single Project. Bus Root, beads instance, and worktree scratch are all scoped to that Project. Loud-fails when the project has no `.beads/` directory. When `bd ready` is empty the mesh idles on its bus socket — no Foremen, no model cost. Wakes automatically when the bd-watcher emits a wake envelope.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #1, #2, #17, #18); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `npm run mesh -- --project <path>` brings up Kanban, bd-watcher, and human-relay scoped to the project
- [ ] Missing `.beads/` directory at the project path → loud failure with a clear error
- [ ] With no ready issues, the mesh stays idle (no Foremen, no model calls observable)
- [ ] Adding a ready bd issue triggers a wake → Kanban dispatch (verified via tmux walkthrough)
- [ ] Bus Root is scoped per project so two projects can run meshes in parallel without collision
- [ ] Header / launch-script references PRD-0001 and ADR-0005

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-cmy (bd-watcher)
- AgentFactory-351 (Kanban script)

title: Kanban script — non-LLM peer with spawn-decision core
description:
## What to build

A new `scripts/kanban.mjs` long-lived non-LLM peer (precedent: `scripts/human-relay.mjs`) that binds a bus socket, receives `wake` envelopes from the bd-watcher, queries bd, and spawns Foremen for ready issues. Spawn logic is a pure function `(bdState, currentForemen, maxConcurrent) → spawnDecisions[]` and is the testable core. Spawn glue invokes `node scripts/run-agent.mjs <foreman-recipe> --issue beads-NNN`. Logs dispatch + alive-Foreman state. Concurrency cap exposed via `--max-concurrent-foremen` flag.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #5, #6, #26); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `scripts/kanban.mjs` binds a bus socket like `human-relay.mjs`
- [ ] Pure spawn-decision function tested with fake bd output covering: empty bd, ready issue, blocked issue, max-concurrent reached, Foreman already alive for issue
- [ ] `--max-concurrent-foremen` flag respected by the spawn-decision function
- [ ] Spawn glue invokes `run-agent.mjs <foreman-recipe> --issue beads-NNN` (recipe configurable; may stub the Foreman recipe in this slice)
- [ ] Logs dispatched issues and the live-Foreman set on each wake
- [ ] Header references PRD-0001 and ADR-0005

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-ndr (deferred-* reorg)

title: bd-watcher extension emits wake envelopes on bd transitions
description:
## What to build

A new `pi-sandbox/.pi/extensions/ralph/bd-watcher.ts` extension that observes the Project's bd state and emits `wake` bus envelopes to a configured target peer (the Kanban) whenever a relevant transition fires (issue-ready, issue-blocked, issue-closed). V1 ships bare-wake-up envelopes — the Kanban re-queries bd on every wake. Transition detection is a pure function over two bd-state snapshots and is the testable core.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #4, #18, #19, #29); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `extensions/ralph/bd-watcher.ts` exists, registered as a normal extension
- [ ] Pure transition-detection function consumes two bd-state snapshots and returns the wake events to emit
- [ ] Unit tests feed fixture snapshots covering: ready issue appears, blocker cleared, issue closed, blocked issue stays invisible
- [ ] Bus glue emits one `wake` envelope per detected transition to a configurable target peer
- [ ] Blocked issues do not produce wake events
- [ ] Header references PRD-0001 and ADR-0005

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-ndr (deferred-* reorg)

title: Worktree manager extension (prepareWorktree / disposeWorktree)
description:
## What to build

A new `pi-sandbox/.pi/extensions/ralph/worktree-mgr.ts` extension that owns the per-issue git worktree lifecycle for a Foreman. Exposes `prepareWorktree(issueId, projectPath) → {worktreePath, branchName}` and `disposeWorktree(worktreePath)` for the Foreman to call at session start and end. Pure-function core (branch-name + worktree-path resolution) is testable without a real repo; lifecycle is testable against a tmpdir git repo.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #8, #27); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `extensions/ralph/worktree-mgr.ts` exists, registered as a normal extension
- [ ] Pure function resolves branch name (e.g., `feature/beads-NNN-<slug>`) and worktree path from `(issueId, projectPath)`
- [ ] `prepareWorktree` runs `git worktree add` on the resolved branch / path
- [ ] `disposeWorktree` runs `git worktree remove` and tolerates already-disposed paths
- [ ] Unit tests cover pure-function resolution
- [ ] Unit tests cover lifecycle against a tmpdir git repo (real git, no model)
- [ ] Header references PRD-0001 and ADR-0005

## Blocked by

- AgentFactory-ndr (deferred-* reorg)

title: Add branch-mode variant to Submission envelope payload
description:
## What to build

Extend the `submission` bus envelope schema with a branch-mode payload variant so a Ralph-Loop Foreman can ship a feature branch as its deliverable. Extend the supervisor inbox action graph to handle branch-mode submissions across the existing approve / reject / revise / escalate surface. Schema and action-graph work only — no Foreman or human-relay UI changes in this slice.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (contributes to Stories #11, #12, #14, #28, #33); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `_lib/bus-envelope.ts` accepts a branch-mode submission payload `{branchRef, projectPath, issueId, testOutput?}`
- [ ] Invalid branch-mode payloads rejected by the schema (negative tests)
- [ ] Existing artifact-list submission payloads still validate (regression)
- [ ] `_lib/supervisor-inbox.ts` action graph handles branch-mode payloads for approve, reject, revise, escalate
- [ ] Unit tests cover schema validation and action-graph routing for branch payloads, mirroring existing artifact-list tests
- [ ] Supervisor extension stays loaded but inert — no V1 LLM in the loop (per ADR-0004)

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

title: Reorg deferred-* stack into deferred/ subdirectories
description:
## What to build

Move the deferred-* extension stack and recipes into `deferred/` subdirectories so the directory layout cleanly separates the existing review-bounded drafting stack from the incoming Ralph-Loop stack. No behaviour change — existing extensions, recipes, and tmux walkthroughs continue to work unchanged.

Source: `docs/prd/0001-ralph-loop-mesh.md` (Stories #22, #23, #24); see also ADR-0005.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pi-sandbox/.pi/extensions/deferred/` contains `deferred-{write,edit,move,delete,confirm}`, `sandbox`, `no-edit`, `atomic-delegate` (and their `.prompt.md` siblings)
- [ ] `pi-sandbox/agents/deferred/` contains `deferred-{writer,author,editor}`, `writer-foreman`, `delegator`, `peer-chatter`, `mesh-{authority,node,writer}`
- [ ] Top-level universal extensions unchanged: `agent-bus`, `habitat`, `supervisor`, `agent-header`, `agent-footer`, `hide-extensions-list`, `no-startup-help`
- [ ] Runner / extension loader resolves the new subdirectory paths
- [ ] `npm test` green
- [ ] `npm run agent -- deferred/deferred-writer` boots; deferred-writer tmux walkthrough from `docs/agents.md` still passes

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
